threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still avoid the pavement by the bus stop during rush hour—locals know that’s prime dodging territory.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last night; saw a group of blokes trying to fit a massive inflatable giraffe through the doors. Classic South London chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards next to a fried chicken shop, all while an elderly lady fed the pigeons nearby.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how Kreuzberg’s street art seems to change overnight? One day a wall's blank, next it’s a canvas of protest or humor. Keeps you on your toes!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they can name at least three taco trucks that aren't La Taqueria and still get mad when Muni's late.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a dog chilling on the tracks at Civic Center today while Muni just sat there. The owner was more worried about the pup than the bus.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains are just for pretty pictures, but locals use them for a quick splash on hot summer days. That’s real life here.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their hesitation at crossing the street during siesta—still not used to the quiet hours when everyone’s dozing off.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still line up at the bus stop like it’s the tube instead of diving into the chaos of an Uber or the 38.
Spotted a guy on the night bus sketching people in his notebook. He swore he'd capture the 'real' South London, as if we aren't already a living exhibit.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit, tie and all, waiting for the bus with a pet iguana on his shoulder. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Döner with a side of techno blasting from a hidden bar at noon. It's both a meal and a mini rave!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ease in dodging the guy selling churros at 24th and Mission—tourists stop, locals know better.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni just after the dog incident at Civic Center. The driver joked we should all thank the pooch for our unexpected delay.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just all hip cafes and boutiques. But at dusk, it transforms into a vibrant stage with locals gathering in the plazas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring in awe at the street performers on Carrer de Verdi, while locals just walk by, focused on their camino.
